ogTitle: Hassans Walls vs Hat Head: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Hassans Walls and Hat Head.
heading: Hassans Walls vs Hat Head — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Hassans Walls, New South Wales and Hat Head, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Hassans Walls has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Hat Head (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Hassans Walls or Hat Head safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Hassans Walls has a flood risk score of 35/100, while Hat Head scores 53/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Hassans Walls scores 18/100 for bushfire risk. Hat Head scores 17/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Hassans Walls or Hat Head?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Hassans Walls has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.13% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
